dialogueChunks.intro: The memorial is finished, {player_name}. The whole settlement gathered for the dedication.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} spoke at the ceremony, the first words they've said in weeks. They talked about the people we lost: not how they died, but how they lived. The farmer who always shared his harvest. The guard who taught the children sword forms with wooden sticks. The elder who remembered every name and every birthday. When the torches were lit and the names read aloud, something shifted in {location}. Not healing exactly, but the beginning of it. The settlement voted unanimously to stay and rebuild.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave us the materials, but more than that, you gave us the space to grieve properly. Take this, and know that every name on that memorial includes yours in spirit.

playerResponses.text: Remembering how they lived matters more than how they died. I'm glad it helped.
A community that mourns together can rebuild together. Take care.

dialogueChunks.intro: She held it to her chest, {player_name}. The moment I gave it to her, she held it and wouldn't let go.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My daughter doesn't understand death yet. She's too young for that. But she understands that the carved figure you recovered smells like her father's workshop and fits in her hand the way it was meant to. She sleeps with it now, and sometimes she whispers to it. {target_npc} says children process grief through objects and rituals, and that this keepsake will become the anchor for every memory she builds of him as she grows. You gave her something no amount of time could replace.
dialogueChunks.outro: He would have liked you, I think. He always admired people who did hard things for simple reasons. Take this, from both of us.

playerResponses.text: A child holding her father's last gift. That image will stay with me.
I'm glad she has something to hold onto. Take care of her.

dialogueChunks.intro: The tower is finished, {player_name}. I set the last stone this morning, right as the sun came up.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I stood on top and looked out over {location} and cried. Not the hopeless kind of crying I've been doing for weeks: the kind that comes when something heavy finally lifts. {target_npc} came up and stood with me, and we didn't say anything for a long time. The tower works: you can see the approaches from every direction, and the guards are already planning watch rotations. My partner would have loved the view. I carved their name into the cornerstone where it will outlast all of us.
dialogueChunks.outro: You brought me the materials, but you also brought me a reason to get out of bed in the morning. That's not a small thing. Take this.

playerResponses.text: A name in stone and a purpose renewed. They'd be proud of what you built.
The best memorials are the ones that keep the living safe. Well done.
